title: Shifting Our Paradigms | HG Amitasana Dasa | SB 4.1.60 | 2019-06-14 |
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: After describing the descendants of the 13 wife of Dharma, who were all daughters of Daksha,
Maitreya now describes the 14th daughter of Daksha, Swaha, and her three sons.
Oblations offered in sacrificial fires are meant for demigods,
and on behalf of demigods, the three sons of Agni and Swaha, namely Pavaka, Pavamana and Shuchi,
We all know Newton was a very God-conscious scientist.
And his friend, who was by and large an atheist,
and whenever they would get into some kind of discussion about God,
And one day when they were discussing about existence of God, Creator, the Supreme Being,
so this friend said, gone are the days when man believed it rained because of rain God.
We have our scriptures, rain God, sun God, fire God, here we are discussing about.
Gone are the days when man believed it rains because of rain God.
Today we are living in a world of science where we have scientific explanation for all the observed phenomena.
Vedas say Indra has to be pleased, when Indra gets pleased, rains come.
This is no, it's very clear now, the rain comes because of clouds, clouds is because of evaporation of water.
There is no gods involved in the whole process. It's a scientific phenomena.
So Newton argued that, you know, even if there is sun because of which the water is getting evaporated,
there is someone who has created the whole system.
Someone who has intelligently, behind any creation there is intelligence which goes in.
So there has to be an intelligent being, an intelligent creator.
And we all know that Newton took his friend to his lab
and there was a three-dimensional model of the solar system
where the sun was there and all the planets moving in their orbits.
So the friend, as soon as he saw that, he says, wonderful, beautiful, who has made it?
So Newton said, it just landed from nowhere.
Yesterday night I was working late in the lab, it was not there.
Today morning when I came, opened the door, I saw this model.
So friend, come on, don't joke, how can this be possible?
Tell me who simulated the model?
So Newton said that just like for you to reconcile the fact that this has come from nowhere is so difficult,
how can you be so confident this wonderful creation with so much of orderliness,
so much of intelligence has just come from nowhere?
